title: Modular house falls off truck during I-495 crash
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News Officials say the three-vehicle crash caused delays in Amesbury during the Monday afternoon commute.
ShareSend this article to your social connections.
A modular home rolled off a truck and onto Interstate 495 in Amesbury Monday afternoon during a three-vehicle crash that caused hours of delays, officials said.

title: Woburn biotech cuts more than 200 jobs after FDA setback
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Business The layoffs follow the agency’s decision not to approve the company’s advanced melanoma treatment.
Replimune is cutting 223 jobs across its headquarters in Woburn and manufacturing facility in Framingham after the FDA failed to approve its latest treatment for advanced melanoma earlier this month.
According to the company, the first round of layoffs was announced on April 10, affecting 63 positions at its commercial organization.

title: Former Cambridge City Councilor Paul Toner gets pretrial probation in brothel case
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Crime Toner’s misdemeanor charge will be dismissed upon his completion of nine months of pretrial probation.
Former Cambridge City Councilor Paul Toner.

Matthew J Lee/The Boston Globe By Abby Patkin April 22, 2026 | 1:57 PM 2 minutes to read A former Cambridge city councilor accused of paying for sex at a Greater Boston brothel ring will serve pretrial probation to resolve his criminal case.

title: Missing Boston hiker found dead on N.H. trail
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News Officials say 61-year-old Kent Wood was unprepared for the 3-5 inches of snow that fell between Sunday and Monday.
The Rim Trail on the summit of Cannon Mountain lends a great view of Franconia Notch State Park.
Lafayette Campground is directly off the parkway in the center of the photo.

title: This runner got engaged at Mile 23 of the Boston Marathon
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Boston Marathon "Two of my dreams came true on the same day.
" Julio Salguero and Erika Sanabria got engaged during the Boston Marathon.
Julio Salguero ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: The Grateful Dead just released a 5-LP box set of a particular classic Boston concert. Here’s why.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Entertainment New Englanders love the Grateful Dead — and the Grateful Dead loved New England, says their archivist.
Jerry Garcia and Bob Weir of The Grateful Dead perform in Europe in 1972.
The band’s new release captures a classic Boston concert from four years later.

title: Passenger on Boston-bound flight had measles, health officials say
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Health The infected individual flew to Logan Airport from Fort Lauderdale last week, the Boston Public Health Commission said.
Passengers use the pedestrian bridge to access Terminal C at Logan Airport in 2021.
Craig F.

title: Car crashes into Somerville dentist’s office, prompting street closure
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Local News Police rerouted traffic near Davis Square as firefighters removed the wrecked vehicle from the building.
Davis Square Dental was crashed into by a vehicle in Somerville early Wednesday morning.

Emma Platoff/Globe Staff A 72-year-old driver crashed his vehicle into the first floor of a dentist’s office off of Davis Square in Somerville on Wednesday morning, prompting road closures and diversions during the early morning commute.

title: Workers detained by ICE at Allston Car Wash allege civil rights violations in new complaint
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Local News "We were just doing our jobs when armed officers surrounded us and treated us like criminals," one worker said.
Allston Car Wash.
